_comment: Doom franchise data file. Covers all games, ports, film adaptations, novels, board games, and significant community works.
description: id Software's landmark first-person shooter franchise (1993–present). Defined the FPS genre with demon-slaying action, heavy metal aesthetics, and push-forward combat. Spawned a massive modding community, multiple console generations, film adaptations, and a 2020s revival that reset the series' timeline.

media.title: Doom
media.description: The original. UAC Mars base, demonic invasion, space marine protagonist. Knee-Deep in the Dead, The Shores of Hell, Inferno episodes. Shareware phenomenon.
media.notes: Revolutionary FPS. Multiple source ports (GZDoom, Chocolate Doom). Thousands of custom WADs. Ultimate Doom (1995) added Thy Flesh Consumed episode.

media.title: Doom II: Hell on Earth
media.description: Direct sequel. Hell invades Earth. Super Shotgun introduced. New enemies: Revenant, Mancubus, Arch-Vile, Pain Elemental, Hell Knight.
media.notes: Added 32 new levels, plus Master Levels (1995) as expansion. Included in Doom Classic Complete.

media.title: Final Doom
media.description: Two 32-level episodes: TNT: Evilution and The Plutonia Experiment. Officially licensed expansion, later bundled as commercial release.
media.notes: Developed by TeamTNT (Evilution) and Dario & Milo Casali (Plutonia). Distributed by id Software. Plutonia significantly harder.

media.title: Doom 64
media.description: Nintendo 64 exclusive. Atmospheric reimagining with original levels, dark ambient soundtrack (Aubrey Hodges). Demonic ritual theme. Sequel to Doom II.
media.notes: Re-released as Doom 64 (2020) for modern platforms with restored Lost Levels. Considered canon post-Doom II.

media.title: Doom (Mobile, 2005)
media.description: J2ME mobile port. 3D engine built for feature phones. Covers Ultimate Doom episodes.
media.notes: DEFUNCT — J2ME era. No longer available. Emulation possible via J2ME Loader.

media.title: Doom RPG
media.description: Turn-based RPG on feature phones. First-person grid movement, resource management, story-driven. Stan Blazkowicz protagonist.
media.notes: DEFUNCT — removed from iOS after 32-bit deprecation. J2ME and Brew versions abandonware. Critical cult classic.

media.title: Doom II RPG
media.description: Sequel to Doom RPG. More complex combat, hacking minigame, new enemy types. Canon storyline.
media.notes: DEFUNCT — same status as Doom RPG. iOS version delisted. Abandonware.

media.title: Doom Resurrection
media.description: iOS exclusive rail shooter. On-rails combat with motion controls. Set between Doom 64 and Doom 3.
media.notes: DEFUNCT — removed from App Store. No longer playable on modern iOS.

media.title: Doom 3
media.description: Reboot/retelling. Horror-focused, slower pace, flashlight mechanic. Mars UAC facility, demonic possession, PDA audio logs.
media.notes: Original version. Resurrection of Evil expansion (2005) added Grabber and new campaign. BFG Edition (2012) reworked.

media.title: Doom 3: BFG Edition
media.description: Doom 3 remaster. Includes Doom 3, Resurrection of Evil, and The Lost Mission (7 new levels). Shoulder-mounted flashlight, improved visuals.
media.notes: The Lost Mission exclusive to BFG Edition. Doom 1 and 2 included on console versions.

media.title: Doom (2016)
media.description: Series reboot. Push-forward combat, glory kills, Argent Energy narrative. Doom Slayer origin. Heavy metal soundtrack by Mick Gordon.
media.notes: Canon reset. Doom Slayer = original Doom marine from classic timeline (confirmed in Eternal). Multiplayer mode, Arcade mode added post-launch.

media.title: Doom Eternal
media.description: Direct sequel to Doom 2016. Expanded mobility (dash, meathook), resource management, sentinel lore. The Ancient Gods expansions.
media.notes: The Ancient Gods — Part One (2020), Part Two (2021). Master Levels, Battlemode (2v1 multiplayer). Horde mode added. Concludes Doom Slayer arc.

media.title: Doom VFR
media.description: VR-exclusive entry. Original campaign set during Doom 2016. First-person teleportation/dash locomotion.
media.notes: Canon placement ambiguous. Requires VR headset. Mixed reception due to locomotion limitations.

media.title: Mighty Doom
media.description: Vertical shooter roguelike. Mini Doom Slayer figure. Run-and-gun through short levels, upgrade loadouts.
media.notes: Launched March 2023, servers shut down August 2024. DEFUNCT — no longer playable. Microtransaction-driven.

media.title: Doom (Novel)
media.description: Novel series by Dafydd ab Hugh and Brad Linaweaver. Knee-Deep in the Dead (1995), Hell on Earth (1995), Infernal Sky (1996), Endgame (1996).
media.notes: Non-canon. Infamously introduces alien technology and alternate dimensions divergent from game canon. Out of print.

media.title: Doom: The Board Game
media.description: FFG board game. One player controls demons, others play marines. Scenario-based. Based on Doom 3 aesthetic.
media.notes: Out of print. Later repurposed mechanics for FFG's Descent: Journeys in the Dark.

media.title: Doom: The Board Game (2016)
media.description: Second FFG board game. Based on Doom 2016 design. Streamlined rules, campaign progression, modular tiles.
media.notes: Out of print. Includes multiple expansions for new demons and marines.

media.title: Doom (2005 Film)
media.description: Universal Pictures adaptation. The Rock (Dwayne Johnson), Karl Urban. Mars research facility, genetic mutation plot rather than demons. First-person sequence.
media.notes: Critical failure. Box office: $58M on $60M budget. No demons, changed to 'genetic mutation' to avoid religious controversy.

media.title: Doom: Annihilation
media.description: Direct-to-video Universal 1440 Entertainment film. Amy Manson, Dominic Mafham. Demons return to canon. Space marine squad, Phobos moon base.
media.notes: Direct-to-DVD/Blu-ray. Low budget. Some demon terminology restored. Mixed fan reception.

media.title: Doom: Endgame
media.description: Fourth novel in the series. Concludes the story with interdimensional conflict and aliens.
media.notes: Part of the 4-novel series. Mass market paperback.

media.title: Doom Comic Book
media.description: Marvel Comics promotional comic. Infamous for dialogue: 'Rip and tear your guts!' 'Big f---ing gun!' Distributed with PC Gamer UK.
media.notes: Single issue. Internet meme status. Written by Steve Behling. Not widely available.

media.title: Masters of Doom
media.description: David Kushner's book. History of id Software and the making of Doom, Quake, and Wolfenstein. John Romero and John Carmack focus.
media.notes: Non-fiction. Essential reading for franchise history. Audiobook available.

media.title: Doom: Knee-Deep in the Dead (Graphic Novel)
media.description: Third-person graphic novel adaptation of Doom Episode 1. Art by Chris D. Szego. Dark Horse Comics.
media.notes: Official licensed graphic novel. One-shot. Not continued.

media.title: Doom (SIGIL)
media.description: John Romero's unofficial 5th episode for Doom (1993). Released on 25th anniversary. 9 levels, Buckethead soundtrack.
media.notes: Fan works / Prestige. Free with optional paid physical edition. Requires Doom II IWAD. Sigil II (2024) released for 30th anniversary.

media.title: SIGIL II
media.description: John Romero's second unofficial episode for Doom II. Released for Doom's 30th anniversary. 9 levels, new enemy variants, Thorr soundtrack.
media.notes: Fan works / Prestige. Free download. Physical collectors edition via Limited Run Games.

media.title: MyHouse.wad
media.description: Cult-favorite Doom II mod. Surrealist horror WAD based on House of Leaves. Explores grief, memory, and impossible architecture.
media.notes: Fan works / Prestige. Won Cacoward. Single level with extensive scripting. Landmark modding achievement.

media.title: Brutal Doom
media.description: Gameplay mod for Doom/Doom II. Adds gore, executions, reloading, modern FPS mechanics. Massive influence on Doom 2016's glory kill system.
media.notes: Fan works / Prestige. Most downloaded Doom mod. Controversial for violence. Multiple versions ongoing.

media.title: Doom: The Dark Ages
media.description: Upcoming prequel to Doom 2016. Sentinels setting, medieval Doom Slayer, shield mechanics, dragon/demon mechs. Announced June 2024, release TBA.
media.notes: Not yet released as of 2026. Presumed canon. Set between Doom 64 and Doom 2016.

media.title: Doom (Ray-Ban Stories)
media.description: Doom 2016 ported to Ray-Ban Stories smart glasses. Single level demo. Discontinued experiment.
media.notes: DEFUNCT — limited promotional release. Not publicly available.

media.title: Doom (Tesla)
media.description: Doom (1993) ported to Tesla infotainment systems. Unofficial port via Steam Deck demo but later supported.
media.notes: Playable on Tesla vehicles via integrated Steam (on supported models).

media.title: Doom (3DO) Music
media.description: 3DO port's soundtrack composed entirely by a single musician (Rebecca Heineman) under duress. Infamous for unusual renditions.
media.notes: Not media; port historical note. Audio tracks preserved.

media.title: Doom (2016) Original Soundtrack
media.description: Mick Gordon's soundtrack. Industrial metal, djent, electronic. BFG Division, Rip & Tear. Nominated for multiple awards.
media.notes: Audiobook / Music. Released via Bethesda, streaming platforms.

media.title: Doom Eternal Original Soundtrack
media.description: Mick Gordon's second soundtrack. Production controversy with Bethesda. The Only Thing They Fear Is You.
media.notes: Audiobook / Music. Streaming release. Mixing dispute led to Mick Gordon not returning for DLC.

media.title: Doom (Pocket PC / Windows Mobile)
media.description: Windows Mobile port of Doom. Commercial release on early PDAs.
media.notes: DEFUNCT. Abandonware.

media.title: Doom (TI-83 Calculator)
media.description: Demake / fan port. Doom running on graphing calculators via assembly. Landmark demoscene achievement.
media.notes: Fan works / Prestige. Multiple versions. Runs at ~6 FPS on monochrome screen.

media.title: Doom: The Lost Episode
media.description: Single level included in Doom 64 (2020) re-release. Connects Doom 64 to Doom (2016).
media.notes: Officially part of Doom 64 re-release. Canon bridge between classic and modern timelines.
